title: Reading and Use of English - Part 7
description: You are going to read an article about four people who talk about their hobbies. For questions 43–52, choose from the people (A–D). The people may be chosen more than once.

content.title: Time Well Spent: Four People Talk About Their Hobbies

texts.A:
### Clara, 28, Graphic Designer
[43]I took up pottery three years ago after feeling completely burned out at work.[/43] I needed something that had absolutely nothing to do with screens. Sitting at the wheel and working with clay forces you to be completely present – you simply can't think about emails or deadlines when you're trying to centre clay. [47]What surprised me most was how quickly I made friends at the local pottery studio.[/47] There's something about sharing a creative space that brings people together naturally. [51]I now sell some of my pieces at a local market, which was never part of the original plan, but it feels good to know others appreciate what I make.[/51]
texts.B:
### James, 35, Teacher
[44]I've been playing chess since I was seven, when my grandfather taught me.[/44] I love that it's a hobby where age or physical ability doesn't matter – what counts is your thinking. [48]Recently, I joined an online chess club and now play against people from all over the world, which has made the hobby feel fresh again.[/48] [52]The only downside is that my family sometimes complains I spend too many evenings staring at my phone playing online games instead of being present.[/52] I try to find a balance, but it's difficult when you're in the middle of a complex match.
texts.C:
### Maria, 42, Nurse
[45]I started running during the pandemic as a way of getting out of the house and clearing my head.[/45] What began as short ten-minute jogs has turned into a serious commitment – I've now completed four half-marathons. [49]I find that running gives me time to think through problems I face at work without actually being at work.[/49] It's a kind of moving meditation. I also follow several running coaches online and have learned a lot about technique and nutrition, although I sometimes feel overwhelmed by how much information is out there.
texts.D:
### Tom, 19, Student
[46]I got into photography after my sister lent me her old camera during a family holiday.[/46] I was amazed by how much you notice about the world when you're looking for something worth photographing. [50]I prefer shooting people in everyday situations rather than landscapes or architecture because I find human stories more interesting.[/50] I've entered a few local competitions and although I haven't won anything, the feedback from judges has helped me improve significantly. I'm currently saving up to buy my own professional camera so I don't have to keep borrowing equipment.

questions.question: began their hobby as a way of escaping the pressure of work?
questions.explanation: Clara started pottery after feeling burned out at work and needing to get away from screens.

questions.question: learnt their hobby from an older family member?
questions.explanation: James says his grandfather taught him chess when he was seven years old.

questions.question: started their hobby as a result of a particular event or period?
questions.explanation: Maria started running during the pandemic as a way to get outside.

questions.question: became interested in their hobby after using someone else's equipment?
questions.explanation: Tom got into photography after borrowing his sister's old camera on a family holiday.

questions.question: was pleasantly surprised to form new friendships through their hobby?
questions.explanation: Clara says she was surprised by how quickly she made friends at the pottery studio.

questions.question: describes reconnecting with a hobby by using technology?
questions.explanation: James joined an online chess club to play internationally, which made the hobby feel fresh again.

questions.question: finds their hobby helps them process difficulties from their professional life?
questions.explanation: Maria says running gives her time to think through work problems without being at work.

questions.question: expresses a preference for a particular type of subject in their hobby?
questions.explanation: Tom prefers photographing people in everyday situations over landscapes or architecture.

questions.question: mentions an unexpected financial benefit from their hobby?
questions.explanation: Clara now sells her pottery at a local market, something she never originally planned to do.

questions.question: acknowledges that their hobby has caused tension with the people they live with?
questions.explanation: James says his family complains he spends too many evenings on his phone playing online chess.
